cards.source: I care about you, and I want to understand how you feel.
cards.target: Me importas y quiero entender cómo te sientes.

backtranslate.resultText: I care about you, and I want to understand how you feel.

cards.source: I want our relationship to feel honest and safe.
cards.target: Quiero que nuestra relación se sienta honesta y segura.

backtranslate.resultText: I want our relationship to feel honest and safe.

cards.source: Please tell me if you need more space tonight.
cards.target: Por favor, dime si necesitas más espacio esta noche.

backtranslate.resultText: Please tell me if you need more space tonight.

cards.source: I respect your answer, even if it is no.
cards.target: Respeto tu respuesta, incluso si es no.

backtranslate.resultText: I respect your answer, even if it is no.

cards.source: He wants to tell her that she matters to him.
cards.target: Él quiere decirle a ella que es importante para él.

backtranslate.resultText: He wants to tell her that she matters to him.

cards.source: He asks her what would make her feel comfortable.
cards.target: Él le pregunta a ella qué la haría sentirse cómoda.

backtranslate.resultText: He asks her what would make her feel comfortable.

cards.source: He promises her that he will not pressure her.
cards.target: Él le promete que no la presionará.

backtranslate.resultText: He promises her that he will not pressure her.

cards.source: He tells her that her privacy is important to him.
cards.target: Él le dice que su privacidad es importante para él.

backtranslate.resultText: He tells her that her privacy is important to him.

cards.source: He would like to spend quiet time with her.
cards.target: A él le gustaría pasar un tiempo tranquilo con ella.

backtranslate.resultText: He would like to spend quiet time with her.

cards.source: He tells her that trust matters more than rushing.
cards.target: Él le dice que la confianza importa más que apresurarse.

backtranslate.resultText: He tells her that trust matters more than rushing.

cards.source: She wants to tell him that she feels close to him.
cards.target: Ella quiere decirle a él que se siente cercana a él.

backtranslate.resultText: She wants to tell him that she feels close to him.

cards.source: She asks him to listen before responding.
cards.target: Ella le pide que escuche antes de responder.

backtranslate.resultText: She asks him to listen before responding.

cards.source: She tells him that affection should include patience.
cards.target: Ella le dice que el afecto debe incluir paciencia.

backtranslate.resultText: She tells him that affection should include patience.

cards.source: She wants him to know that she values honesty.
cards.target: Ella quiere que él sepa que valora la honestidad.

backtranslate.resultText: She wants him to know that she values honesty.

cards.source: She tells him that she needs time to think.
cards.target: Ella le dice que necesita tiempo para pensar.

backtranslate.resultText: She tells him that she needs time to think.

cards.source: She invites him to share dinner and talk calmly.

backtranslate.resultText: Please show me how to fry an egg.

cards.source: I would like to try local street food.
cards.target: Me gustaría probar la comida callejera local.

backtranslate.resultText: I would like to try local street food.

cards.source: I would like to try northern regional food.
cards.target: Me gustaría probar la comida regional del norte.

backtranslate.resultText: I would like to try northern regional food.

cards.source: I would like to try southern regional food.
cards.target: Me gustaría probar la comida regional del sur.

backtranslate.resultText: I would like to try southern regional food.

cards.source: The baker opened his shop before sunrise. He noticed that the old clock had stopped, but the bread still rose on time. By noon, neighbors came in worried about their own delays. He smiled and said that a steady hand matters more than a perfect clock.
cards.target: El panadero abrió su tienda antes del amanecer. Notó que el viejo reloj se había detenido, pero el pan aún subió a tiempo. Al mediodía, los vecinos llegaron preocupados por sus propios retrasos. Él sonrió y dijo que una mano constante importa más que un reloj perfecto.

backtranslate.resultText: The baker opened his shop before sunrise. He noticed that the old clock had stopped, but the bread still rose on time. By noon, neighbors came in worried about their own delays. He smiled and said that a steady hand matters more than a perfect clock.

cards.source: A narrow bridge crossed the river behind the school. Each day, two students passed without speaking. One afternoon, the rain made the boards slippery, and one student offered a hand. After that, the bridge was still quiet, but it no longer felt lonely.
cards.target: Un puente estrecho cruzaba el río detrás de la escuela. Cada día, dos estudiantes pasaban sin hablar. Una tarde, la lluvia volvió resbaladizas las tablas y uno de ellos ofreció la mano. Después de eso, el puente siguió siendo silencioso, pero ya no se sintió solitario.

backtranslate.resultText: A narrow bridge crossed the river behind the school. Each day, two students passed without speaking. One afternoon, the rain made the boards slippery, and one student offered a hand. After that, the bridge was still quiet, but it no longer felt lonely.

cards.source: Only one mango remained on the table. The siblings both wanted it and both pretended not to. Their grandmother cut it into thin slices and placed them on one plate. They learned that sharing did not make the fruit smaller; it made the afternoon larger.
cards.target: Solo quedaba un mango sobre la mesa. Los hermanos lo querían y ambos fingían que no. Su abuela lo cortó en rebanadas finas y las puso en un solo plato. Aprendieron que compartir no hacía más pequeña la fruta; hacía más grande la tarde.

backtranslate.resultText: Only one mango remained on the table. The siblings both wanted it and both pretended not to. Their grandmother cut it into thin slices and placed them on one plate. They learned that sharing did not make the fruit smaller; it made the afternoon larger.

cards.source: The map was old and missed three new roads. A traveler complained until a child pointed to the river. The river had not moved, and neither had the hills. The traveler followed what was still true and reached the town before dark.
cards.target: El mapa era viejo y le faltaban tres caminos nuevos. Un viajero se quejó hasta que un niño señaló el río. El río no se había movido, ni tampoco las colinas. El viajero siguió lo que aún era cierto y llegó al pueblo antes de que oscureciera.

backtranslate.resultText: The map was old and missed three new roads. A traveler complained until a child pointed to the river. The river had not moved, and neither had the hills. The traveler followed what was still true and reached the town before dark.

cards.source: A seed fell beside a stone wall. It could not see the garden beyond the wall, but it could feel the morning rain. Slowly it sent roots into the dark soil. When spring came, its leaves rose high enough to see the garden for themselves.
cards.target: Una semilla cayó junto a un muro de piedra. No podía ver el jardín al otro lado del muro, pero podía sentir la lluvia de la mañana. Poco a poco envió raíces al suelo oscuro. Cuando llegó la primavera, sus hojas crecieron lo suficiente para ver el jardín por sí mismas.

backtranslate.resultText: A seed fell beside a stone wall. It could not see the garden beyond the wall, but it could feel the morning rain. Slowly it sent roots into the dark soil. When spring came, its leaves rose high enough to see the garden for themselves.

cards.source: The merchant promised fair weights to every customer. One day, a rich buyer offered extra money for a false scale. The merchant refused and lost the sale. Years later, the town trusted his shop because his promise had cost him something.
cards.target: El comerciante prometió pesos justos a cada cliente. Un día, un comprador rico ofreció dinero extra por una balanza falsa. El comerciante se negó y perdió la venta. Años después, el pueblo confió en su tienda porque su promesa le había costado algo.

backtranslate.resultText: The merchant promised fair weights to every customer. One day, a rich buyer offered extra money for a false scale. The merchant refused and lost the sale. Years later, the town trusted his shop because his promise had cost him something.

cards.source: A student complained that the classroom window was too small. The teacher asked him to describe one cloud in exact detail. The student looked carefully and filled a page. He learned that attention can enlarge even the smallest view.
cards.target: Un estudiante se quejó de que la ventana del aula era demasiado pequeña. El maestro le pidió que describiera una nube con detalle exacto. El estudiante miró con cuidado y llenó una página. Aprendió que la atención puede agrandar incluso la vista más pequeña.

backtranslate.resultText: A student complained that the classroom window was too small. The teacher asked him to describe one cloud in exact detail. The student looked carefully and filled a page. He learned that attention can enlarge even the smallest view.

cards.source: A fisherman found a receipt floating near his boat. It listed rice, oil, and medicine, but no name. He carried it to the market and asked quietly. By evening, an old woman had her missing package back, and the fisherman had a new friend.
cards.target: Un pescador encontró un recibo flotando cerca de su bote. Enumeraba arroz, aceite y medicina, pero no tenía nombre. Lo llevó al mercado y preguntó discretamente. Al anochecer, una anciana recuperó su paquete perdido y el pescador ganó una nueva amiga.

backtranslate.resultText: A fisherman found a receipt floating near his boat. It listed rice, oil, and medicine, but no name. He carried it to the market and asked quietly. By evening, an old woman had her missing package back, and the fisherman had a new friend.

cards.source:
Lanterns fade above the rice.
Steam climbs from bowls and tea.
Coins ring softly in careful hands.
The day begins by feeding the street.
cards.target:
Los faroles se apagan sobre el arroz.
El vapor sube de cuencos y té.
Las monedas suenan suaves en manos cuidadosas.
El día empieza alimentando la calle.

backtranslate.resultText:
Lanterns fade above the rice.
Steam climbs from bowls and tea.
Coins ring softly in careful hands.
The day begins by feeding the street.

cards.source:
The road keeps a silver memory.
Leaves shake light from their sleeves.
A child steps over a shining puddle.
The sky repairs itself in blue.
cards.target:
El camino guarda una memoria de plata.
Las hojas sacuden luz de sus mangas.
Un niño salta sobre un charco brillante.
El cielo se repara en azul.

backtranslate.resultText:
The road keeps a silver memory.
Leaves shake light from their sleeves.
A child steps over a shining puddle.
The sky repairs itself in blue.

cards.source:
A knife rests beside green herbs.
Soup answers the quiet flame.
Someone hums without knowing the tune.
Home gathers around the spoon.
cards.target:
Un cuchillo descansa junto a hierbas verdes.
La sopa responde a la llama tranquila.
Alguien tararea sin conocer la melodía.
El hogar se reúne alrededor de la cuchara.

backtranslate.resultText:
A knife rests beside green herbs.
Soup answers the quiet flame.
Someone hums without knowing the tune.
Home gathers around the spoon.

cards.source:
Two voices lower, then meet.
The table keeps their open hands.
No one wins the room alone.
Peace signs its name in silence.
cards.target:
Dos voces bajan y luego se encuentran.
La mesa guarda sus manos abiertas.
Nadie gana la habitación a solas.
La paz firma su nombre en silencio.

backtranslate.resultText:
Two voices lower, then meet.
The table keeps their open hands.
No one wins the room alone.
Peace signs its name in silence.

cards.source:
The platform waits with yellow eyes.
Suitcases lean into goodbye.
A whistle cuts the evening clean.
Hope boards before the passengers.
cards.target:
El andén espera con ojos amarillos.
Las maletas se inclinan hacia el adiós.
Un silbato corta limpia la tarde.
La esperanza sube antes que los pasajeros.

backtranslate.resultText:
The platform waits with yellow eyes.
Suitcases lean into goodbye.
A whistle cuts the evening clean.
Hope boards before the passengers.

cards.source:
Numbers sleep in narrow lines.
A careful thumb turns every page.
The debt of kindness has no column.
Still, the heart keeps perfect books.
cards.target:
Los números duermen en líneas estrechas.
Un pulgar cuidadoso pasa cada página.
La deuda de la bondad no tiene columna.
Aun así, el corazón lleva libros perfectos.

backtranslate.resultText:
Numbers sleep in narrow lines.
A careful thumb turns every page.
The debt of kindness has no column.
Still, the heart keeps perfect books.

cards.source:
Glass catches the patient star.
Questions bloom under white light.
The answer waits without hurry.
Truth has learned to breathe slowly.
cards.target:
El vidrio atrapa la estrella paciente.
Las preguntas florecen bajo luz blanca.
La respuesta espera sin prisa.
La verdad ha aprendido a respirar despacio.

backtranslate.resultText:
Glass catches the patient star.
Questions bloom under white light.
The answer waits without hurry.
Truth has learned to breathe slowly.

cards.source:
Every village folds flavor differently.
Pepper remembers one road.
Rice remembers another rain.
Together they call the traveler home.
cards.target:
Cada pueblo dobla el sabor de manera distinta.
La pimienta recuerda un camino.
El arroz recuerda otra lluvia.
Juntos llaman al viajero a casa.

backtranslate.resultText:
Every village folds flavor differently.
Pepper remembers one road.
Rice remembers another rain.
Together they call the traveler home.

catalogs.name: English to Spanish Default
catalogs.desc: TalkBridge canonical phrasebook: English to Spanish. Includes 200 core cards plus 16 bonus literature and poem cards.
